metadata.description: Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) and Ayurvedic dream interpretation knowledge base for AI dream analysis

classical_chinese: Huangdi Neijing Ling Shu (黄帝内经灵枢) - Chapter 43: Excess Evils Release Dreams (淫邪发梦)
Huangdi Neijing Su Wen (黄帝内经素问) - Chapter 17: Treatise on Diagnostic Methods of the Pulse
Huangdi Neijing Su Wen (黄帝内经素问) - Chapter 80: Discussion on Fang Sheng Shuai
Unschuld, Paul U. (2016) - Huang Di Nei Jing Ling Shu: The Ancient Classic on Needle Therapy, UC Press
tcm_clinical: Maciocia, Giovanni - The Foundations of Chinese Medicine (3rd ed.)
Deadman, Peter - A Manual of Acupuncture
Bensky, Dan & Gamble, Andrew - Chinese Herbal Medicine: Materia Medica

metadata.disclaimer: This knowledge base is for educational and interpretive purposes only. It does not constitute medical advice. Users experiencing persistent sleep disturbances should consult a qualified healthcare practitioner.

tcm_dream_theory.summary: In TCM, dreams are not random events but reflections of the body's internal state. The Huangdi Neijing teaches that organ excess (shi) or deficiency (xu) disturbs the five spirits (wu shen) housed in the organs, producing characteristic dream content. Undisturbed dreamless sleep indicates balanced organ function and harmonious circulation of qi and blood.

key_concepts.role: Governs dream activity itself. When the Hun wanders excessively during sleep, vivid or disturbing dreams result.
Governs consciousness. When Shen is unsettled, sleep is restless with many dreams and nightmares.
Governs thought and concentration. When Yi is disturbed, dreams become repetitive and obsessive.
Governs bodily sensations and instincts. When Po is disturbed, dreams involve weeping, grief, and somatic sensations.
Governs willpower and survival instinct. When Zhi is depleted, dreams involve fear, drowning, and existential threats.

patterns.dream_content: Wading through deep water, flooding, immersion; accompanied by fear
Large fires burning, intense heat, volcanic eruptions

patterns.condition_en: Both yin and yang in excess
patterns.dream_content: Fighting, mutual killing, warfare, violent conflict

excess_pattern.pattern_en: Liver Qi Excess / Liver Yang Rising
dream_themes: Intense anger in dreams, arguing, shouting
Frustration and feeling blocked or trapped
dream_examples: Dreaming of furious arguments with no resolution
Dreaming of being trapped in a room and needing to break out
Dreaming of storms, strong winds, violent weather
Dreaming of flying upward uncontrollably
clinical_signs: Headaches, especially at the temples or vertex

dream_themes: Lying immobilized under a tree, unable to rise
Feelings of paralysis or inability to act
dream_examples: Dreaming of lying beneath a tree and being unable to get up
Dreaming of dense forests with fragrant mushrooms
Dreaming of wilted gardens or dying plants
Dreaming of being buried alive

dream_themes: Feeling stuck in traffic or in a maze
clinical_signs: Distending pain in the flanks or chest

beneficial_foods: Green leafy vegetables (spinach, kale, dandelion greens)
Sour foods in moderation (lemon, vinegar, plum)
Goji berries (枸杞 gouqi) to nourish Liver blood
Chrysanthemum tea (菊花茶 juhua cha) to clear Liver heat
Green tea in moderation

lifestyle: Sleep before 11 PM to allow Liver blood regeneration (Liver time: 1-3 AM)
Morning stretching or yoga to move Liver qi
Express emotions constructively rather than suppressing anger
Walk in nature, especially among trees in springtime
Practice deep breathing to release tension

acupressure_points.location: On the top of the foot, in the depression between the 1st and 2nd metatarsal bones, about 2 finger-widths from the web margin
acupressure_points.technique: Press firmly with thumb for 1-2 minutes per side. Breathe deeply.
acupressure_points.indication: Moves Liver qi, calms anger, relieves headaches and irritability

acupressure_points.location: Directly below the nipple, in the 6th intercostal space
acupressure_points.technique: Gentle circular massage for 1 minute per side
acupressure_points.indication: Relieves Liver qi stagnation, chest tightness, and emotional constraint

wellness_recommendations.emotional_awareness: Liver dreams often reflect unexpressed anger, frustration, or creative energy seeking an outlet. Ask yourself: Where in my life do I feel stuck or blocked? What am I not expressing? The Liver governs the smooth flow of qi -- stagnation of emotions directly disturbs it.

dream_themes: Being burned or surrounded by fire
dream_examples: Dreaming of laughing wildly and being unable to stop
Dreaming of houses or cities on fire
Dreaming of blinding light or explosions
Dreaming of volcanic eruptions (especially in summer)

dream_themes: Nightmares and frequent waking from fear
Searching for something that cannot be found
dream_examples: Dreaming of trying to put out a fire that keeps spreading
Dreaming of smoke-filled mountains
Waking repeatedly from disturbing dreams, unable to recall specifics
Dreaming of being lost in a confusing, shifting landscape

beneficial_foods: Bitter foods to clear Heart heat (bitter melon, arugula, dark chocolate in moderation)
Red foods to nourish Heart blood (red dates/jujube 红枣, beets, goji berries)
Lotus seed tea (莲子茶) to calm the Shen
Lily bulb (百合) in soups for Heart yin
Longan fruit (龙眼) to nourish Heart blood

lifestyle: Practice meditation or mindfulness daily to settle the Shen
Limit screen exposure 1 hour before sleep (blue light agitates Heart Fire)
Cultivate a consistent bedtime routine
Journaling before bed to release mental overactivity
Gentle evening walks rather than intense exercise

acupressure_points.location: On the wrist crease, on the ulnar side (pinky side), in the depression at the radial side of the pisiform bone
acupressure_points.technique: Press gently with thumb for 1-2 minutes before bed. Breathe slowly and focus on calming the mind.
acupressure_points.indication: Primary point for calming the Shen, treating insomnia, anxiety, nightmares, and dream-disturbed sleep

acupressure_points.location: On the inner forearm, 2 inches above the wrist crease, between the two tendons
acupressure_points.technique: Press with thumb for 1-2 minutes per side. Useful when feeling anxious before sleep.
acupressure_points.indication: Calms the Heart, unbinds the chest, alleviates palpitations and anxiety

wellness_recommendations.emotional_awareness: Heart dreams often reflect the state of your Shen -- your consciousness, joy, and emotional connection. Excessive fire dreams may indicate overstimulation, unprocessed emotional excitement, or anxiety masquerading as joy. Ask yourself: Am I truly at peace, or am I running on nervous energy? Is my joy authentic or frantic?

dream_themes: Storms with rain leaking into buildings
dream_examples: Dreaming of singing while feeling a strange heaviness
Dreaming of trudging through mud or marsh
Dreaming of a roof leaking during a storm
Dreaming of performing the same task endlessly
clinical_signs: Feeling of heaviness in limbs and head

dream_themes: Hunger, searching for food and not finding it
Building houses or constructing things (especially in late summer)
dream_examples: Dreaming of starving with no food available
Dreaming of constructing a building that never gets finished
Dreaming of an endless to-do list with mounting tasks
Dreaming of falling into a deep ravine or abyss

beneficial_foods: Warm, cooked foods (congee/rice porridge 粥, soups, stews)
Sweet root vegetables (sweet potato, pumpkin, squash, yam 山药)
Ginger tea (姜茶) to warm the Spleen
Chinese red dates (红枣 hong zao) to tonify qi
Millet porridge, oats
Small amounts of honey
foods_to_avoid: Cold, raw foods (salads, smoothies, ice cream)

lifestyle: Eat regular meals at consistent times; avoid skipping meals
Do not eat while distracted (reading, phone, TV)
Gentle post-meal walks to aid digestion
Practice grounding exercises: walking barefoot on earth
Set boundaries on mental work; take thinking breaks
Limit exposure to information overload

acupressure_points.location: On the inner leg, 3 inches (about 4 finger-widths) above the ankle bone, behind the tibia
acupressure_points.technique: Press with thumb for 1-2 minutes per side. Avoid during pregnancy.
acupressure_points.indication: Tonifies Spleen, calms the mind, nourishes blood and yin. One of the most important points for insomnia and dream-disturbed sleep.

acupressure_points.location: On the outer leg, 3 inches below the kneecap, one finger-width lateral to the shinbone
acupressure_points.technique: Press firmly or massage in circles for 2-3 minutes per side. Can be done daily.
acupressure_points.indication: Strengthens Spleen and Stomach qi, boosts overall energy, resolves dampness

wellness_recommendations.emotional_awareness: Spleen dreams often reflect overthinking, worry, and the mind's inability to digest life experiences. Repetitive dreams are a hallmark. Ask yourself: What am I ruminating on? Am I nourishing myself adequately -- not just with food, but with rest and care? Where do I need to let go of excessive mental processing?

dream_themes: Ascending or being lifted upward unwillingly
dream_examples: Dreaming of sobbing uncontrollably
Dreaming of battlefields and carnage
Dreaming of being lifted into the sky against your will
Dreaming of terrifying encounters
clinical_signs: Feeling of oppression in the chest

dream_themes: Strange objects made of gold or iron (Metal element imagery)
Murderous or violent scenes observed passively
dream_examples: Dreaming of flying and seeing wondrous metallic objects
Dreaming of a landscape drained of color, all white
Dreaming of witnessing violence without being able to intervene
Dreaming of suffocating or gasping for air

beneficial_foods: White foods to nourish the Lung (pear 梨, white fungus/tremella 银耳, lily bulb 百合, lotus root 莲藕)
Honey-steamed pear for dry cough and Lung dryness
Almond milk or apricot kernel (杏仁) tea
Astragalus tea (黄芪茶 huangqi cha) to strengthen Lung qi and immunity
Radish soup (萝卜汤) to descend Lung qi
foods_to_avoid: Very spicy foods in excess (small amounts are okay to open the Lung)

lifestyle: Practice deep breathing exercises or qigong daily
Spend time in fresh air; morning walks are ideal
Allow yourself to grieve and release sadness; suppressed grief injures the Lung
Maintain a clean, well-ventilated sleeping environment
Autumn: go to bed early, rise early with the roosters (Su Wen Chapter 2)

acupressure_points.location: On the inner forearm, 1.5 inches above the wrist crease, above the radial artery
acupressure_points.technique: Press with thumbnail for 1 minute per side. Also benefits the head and neck.
acupressure_points.indication: Opens and descends Lung qi, releases the exterior, benefits the head. Key point for grief-related conditions.

acupressure_points.location: On the chest, 1 inch below the clavicle, 6 inches lateral to the midline
acupressure_points.technique: Gentle circular massage for 1-2 minutes. Avoid deep pressure.
acupressure_points.indication: Opens the chest, disperses Lung qi, relieves cough and chest oppression

wellness_recommendations.emotional_awareness: Lung dreams often surface during times of loss, grief, or transition. The Metal element governs letting go -- the Lung and Large Intestine release what is no longer needed. Ask yourself: What am I holding onto that needs to be released? Is there unprocessed grief? Am I allowing myself space to mourn and then move forward?

dream_themes: Spine or lumbar detachment from the body
Disconnection of upper and lower body
dream_examples: Dreaming that your spine is separating from your body
Dreaming of being pulled underwater by a strong current
Dreaming of your lower back dissolving or disconnecting
clinical_signs: Feeling of heaviness in lower body

dream_themes: Overlooking an abyss, standing at the edge of a cliff
Being chased by threats (Kidney Qi deficiency)
Losing physical control, unable to move limbs
dream_examples: Dreaming of plunging into deep water and being terrified
Dreaming of swimming desperately after a shipwreck
Dreaming of standing at the edge of a great abyss
Dreaming of being chased by unknown dark forces
Dreaming of legs giving way, unable to run
clinical_signs: Premature aging, hair loss, graying hair

beneficial_foods: Black foods to nourish Kidney essence (black sesame 黑芝麻, black beans 黑豆, black rice 黑米, blackberries)
Walnuts (核桃) to tonify Kidney yang and strengthen the brain
Bone broth for Kidney Jing
Goji berry and chrysanthemum tea (枸杞菊花茶)
Warm lamb or venison soups in winter
Seaweed, kelp, sea vegetables
foods_to_avoid: Cold, raw foods especially in winter

lifestyle: Go to bed early in winter; conserve energy (Su Wen Chapter 2: rise late, await sunlight)
Avoid overwork and excessive physical or sexual exhaustion
Practice stillness: meditation, tai chi, gentle qigong
Keep the lower back and feet warm
Rub the soles of the feet before bed to connect with Kidney meridian
Moderate exercise; avoid excessive sweating which depletes Kidney yin

acupressure_points.location: On the sole of the foot, in the depression when the toes are curled, approximately at the junction of the anterior 1/3 and posterior 2/3 of the sole
acupressure_points.technique: Rub vigorously with palm for 2-3 minutes per foot before sleep. This draws excess yang downward and calms the mind.
acupressure_points.indication: Anchors the spirit, calms fear and anxiety, treats insomnia from Kidney deficiency, clears heat from the head

acupressure_points.location: On the inner ankle, in the depression between the medial malleolus and the Achilles tendon
acupressure_points.technique: Press gently for 1-2 minutes per side.
acupressure_points.indication: Tonifies Kidney qi and yin, strengthens the lower back, treats fear-based insomnia

wellness_recommendations.emotional_awareness: Kidney dreams often emerge during periods of deep fear, existential anxiety, or physical exhaustion. The Kidneys store Jing (essence) -- our constitutional vitality. Ask yourself: Am I running on empty? What am I deeply afraid of? Am I honoring my need for rest and restoration? Fear is the Kidney's emotion -- acknowledging it rather than suppressing it allows the Kidney energy to stabilize.

deficiency_dreams: Waking early in the morning (3-5 AM) with anxiety
secondary_organs.clinical_note: Gallbladder deficiency often manifests as timidity, indecisiveness, and being easily startled awake.

deficiency_dreams: Dreams of having a large, satisfying meal
excess_dreams: Vivid dreams or nightmares from overeating before bed
Digestive stagnation causing restless, chaotic dreams
secondary_organs.clinical_note: The Stomach is particularly affected by late-night eating. Food stagnation generates heat that disturbs the Shen.

deficiency_dreams: Dreams of large cities, busy streets
Dreams of crowded, overwhelming urban environments
Dreams of open fields, vast empty landscapes

balanced: Dreams of growth, new beginnings, spring landscapes
Planning and organizing, seeing clear paths forward
deficiency: Paralysis, inability to move or act

five_elements_dream_themes.interpretation_guidance: Wood-element dreams reflect your capacity for vision, planning, and assertive action. They speak to whether your life force is flowing smoothly or blocked.

excess: Fires burning out of control, explosions

five_elements_dream_themes.interpretation_guidance: Fire-element dreams reflect the state of your Shen -- your consciousness, emotional connections, and capacity for joy. They reveal whether your inner flame is balanced, raging, or dimming.

balanced: Nurturing, caring for others, feeding people
excess: Feeling heavy, sinking, stuck in mud
deficiency: Falling into deep holes or abysses

five_elements_dream_themes.interpretation_guidance: Earth-element dreams reflect your sense of security, nourishment, and how well you process and digest -- both food and life experiences. They speak to your center of gravity.

balanced: Letting go with grace, autumn leaves falling
deficiency: Witnessing violence without power to intervene

five_elements_dream_themes.interpretation_guidance: Metal-element dreams reflect your relationship with loss, boundaries, and what you value. They ask whether you can let go of what has passed and find clarity in what remains.

balanced: Calm deep water, still lakes, gentle flow
deficiency: Standing at the edge of an abyss
Legs unable to move, frozen in terror

five_elements_dream_themes.interpretation_guidance: Water-element dreams reflect your deepest reserves of vitality, your relationship with fear, and your connection to ancestral wisdom. They reveal whether your foundation is strong or depleted.

seasonal_dream_patterns.source: Huangdi Neijing Su Wen, Chapter 2 (四气调神大论 - Treatise on Regulating the Spirit According to the Four Seasons)
seasonal_dream_patterns.principle: Dreams naturally shift with the seasons as qi flows through different organ systems. Each season activates its corresponding organ, making dreams related to that organ more common and diagnostically significant during its season.

normal_dream_tendencies: Dreams become more vivid and active as yang rises
Dreams of growth, new projects, travel
Increased dream recall compared to winter
More movement and action in dreams
pathological_dreams: Liver deficiency in spring: lying under trees unable to rise
Excessive anger dreams signal Liver yang rising with seasonal qi
Wind-related dreams (storms, tornadoes) reflect external Wind pathogens
seasons.lifestyle_guidance: Go to bed later and rise earlier. Stretch and move freely. Allow your creative energy to flow without constraint. This is the time to begin new endeavors.
seasons.sleep_timing: Sleep: ~11 PM. Wake: ~6 AM (rise with the sun)

normal_dream_tendencies: Dreams may be more emotional and social
Lighter sleep due to longer days, more dream interruptions
Dreams of warmth, joy, celebration, connection
Possible increase in vivid dreams from Heart activity
pathological_dreams: Heart Fire in summer: volcanic eruptions, extreme fire imagery
Heat disturbing Shen: restless, fragmented, incoherent dreams
Anxiety dreams signal Heart yin failing to anchor the Shen
seasons.lifestyle_guidance: Go to bed later and rise earlier. Be active and outgoing. Avoid anger and frustration. Nap at midday if needed to protect the Heart.
seasons.sleep_timing: Sleep: ~11 PM. Wake: ~5:30 AM. Optional midday nap: 20-30 min.

normal_dream_tendencies: Dreams become more grounded, domestic, food-related
Processing and digesting recent experiences
Dreams of home, family, nourishment
Transition dreams as seasons shift
pathological_dreams: Spleen deficiency: hunger dreams, endless construction
Dampness accumulation: swampy, heavy, stuck dreams
Overthinking manifesting as repetitive loop dreams
seasons.lifestyle_guidance: Maintain regularity in eating and sleeping. Center yourself through grounding practices. This is a time for integration and harvest.

normal_dream_tendencies: Dreams become more reflective, introspective
Themes of letting go, separation, transition
May process grief and loss from the year
Decreased dream intensity as yin increases
pathological_dreams: Lung deficiency in autumn: battles, warfare imagery
Grief dreams, crying in dreams signal Lung qi imbalance
Dreams of white objects, metallic themes
seasons.lifestyle_guidance: Go to bed early and rise early. Pull energy inward. Practice letting go of what no longer serves you. Keep the lungs moist in dry weather.
seasons.sleep_timing: Sleep: ~10 PM. Wake: ~6 AM (rise with the roosters).

normal_dream_tendencies: Deepest sleep with less dream recall
When dreams occur, they tend to be deeper and more symbolic
Themes of depth, darkness, inner wisdom
Ancestral or past-life themed dreams more common
pathological_dreams: Kidney deficiency in winter: drowning, shipwreck dreams
Fear-based chase dreams signal depleted Kidney qi
seasons.lifestyle_guidance: Go to bed early and rise late, waiting for sunlight. Conserve energy. Avoid excessive sweating or overexertion. This is the time for deep rest and restoration.

tcm_patterns.description: Lung deficiency causes dreams of flying upward and seeing strange metallic objects. The Lung qi fails to descend, and qi rises unanchored.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Flying feels effortless, may see gold/iron objects, white or pale imagery

tcm_patterns.description: When qi accumulates in the upper body, flying dreams result. This is a general yang excess pattern.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Sensation of lightness in upper body, may feel head pressure upon waking

tcm_patterns.description: Liver yang surging upward can produce soaring dreams. Often accompanied by feelings of exhilaration or anger.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Flying with emotional intensity, may be accompanied by wind or storms

follow_up_questions: Was the flying effortless or were you struggling?
Did you see any metallic objects or white imagery while flying?
How did you feel -- free and joyful, or anxious and unmoored?
Do you tend to have headaches or feel light-headed during the day?

tcm_patterns.description: The Kidney governs water. When Kidney qi is weak, dreams of drowning, shipwrecks, and terrifying water immersion occur.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Fear is prominent, water is deep and dark, feeling of helplessness

tcm_patterns.description: General yin excess produces dreams of wading through great water with fear. The body is overwhelmed by cold, damp yin energy.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Cold water, flooding, sense of being overwhelmed

follow_up_questions: Was the water calm or threatening?
Were you drowning or swimming?
Was the water cold, warm, deep, or shallow?
Do you experience lower back pain, frequent urination, or fatigue?

tcm_patterns.description: Excess Heart fire produces dreams of large fires, eruptions, and burning. The Shen is agitated by internal heat.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Fires are large and out of control, feelings of agitation or excitement

tcm_patterns.description: Heart deficiency produces dreams of attempting to extinguish fires, or seeing mountains with fire and smoke. The dreamer tries to control fire but cannot.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Trying to put out fire, smoke imagery, fire is smaller but persistent

tcm_patterns.description: General yang excess produces dreams of great fires burning. This is a systemic heat pattern, not organ-specific.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Widespread fire, intense heat sensation, may wake feeling hot

follow_up_questions: Was the fire threatening you or were you observing it?
Were you trying to extinguish the fire?
Did you feel hot or sweaty upon waking?
Do you experience palpitations, mouth ulcers, or restlessness?

tcm_patterns.description: When Kidney qi is depleted, the Zhi (willpower) weakens and fear dominates. The dreamer cannot confront the pursuer and can only flee.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Pursuer is unknown/shadowy, legs feel heavy or frozen, pure terror

tcm_patterns.description: Stagnant Liver qi generates a feeling of being trapped. The chase reflects an inability to process and move through frustration.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Pursuer may be known, feelings of frustration mixed with fear, anger upon waking

follow_up_questions: Could you see what was chasing you?
Did your legs work, or were they heavy/frozen?
Did you feel more fearful or more frustrated?
Do you feel exhausted or running on empty in daily life?

tcm_patterns.pattern: Qi Abundant Below / Sinking Qi (下盛 / 气陷)
tcm_patterns.description: When qi is heavy in the lower body or sinks downward, falling dreams occur. This can relate to both Kidney and Spleen qi sinking.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Sudden drops, falling into an abyss or void

tcm_patterns.description: The Kidney anchors the body's foundational qi. When depleted, the dreamer loses their grounding and plummets.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Falling into deep water or dark voids, existential dread

tcm_patterns.description: Spleen qi failing to hold things up produces dreams of falling into abysses, deep holes, or collapsing structures.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Falling into holes, ground giving way, buildings collapsing

follow_up_questions: Were you falling into water, a void, or from a structure?
Did the ground give way beneath you?
Do you experience fatigue, heaviness, or prolapse symptoms?
Do you feel unstable or ungrounded in daily life?

common_dream_themes_diagnosis.theme_zh: 愤怒 / 打斗 / 争吵

tcm_patterns.description: When Liver qi is in excess, the Hun is agitated and dreams of intense anger, physical fighting, and confrontation result. This is one of the most clearly stated correspondences in the Su Wen.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Strong anger, physical violence, confrontation with specific people

tcm_patterns.description: Liver fire blazing upward produces explosive rage in dreams, often with shouting and destructive impulses.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Explosive quality, red imagery, waking with headache or bitter taste

follow_up_questions: Were you the aggressor or the victim?
Was it someone you know?
Did you wake feeling angry or frustrated?
Are you suppressing frustration in your waking life?

tcm_patterns.description: When Lung qi is in excess, the Po is overwhelmed and dreams of weeping, sorrow, and terror result.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Intense crying, feeling of chest oppression in the dream

tcm_patterns.description: Lung deficiency may produce a sense of suffocation, struggling for breath, or passive observation of sad events.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Observing sadness without being able to help, breathlessness in dreams

follow_up_questions: Were you crying yourself or witnessing someone else cry?
Could you breathe easily in the dream?
Have you experienced a recent loss or are you suppressing grief?
Do you have any respiratory symptoms (cough, shortness of breath)?

common_dream_themes_diagnosis.theme_zh: 进食 / 饥饿 / 食物

tcm_patterns.description: When the Spleen is deficient, the body feels undernourished. Dreams of desperate hunger and searching for food that cannot be found reflect this internal starvation.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Searching for food, unable to eat, food disappears or is unsatisfying

tcm_patterns.description: Stomach deficiency produces dreams of lavish meals and feasts -- the body's attempt to compensate for what it lacks.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Large satisfying meals, banquets, abundance of food

follow_up_questions: Were you eating or searching for food?
Was the food satisfying or did it disappear?
Do you skip meals or have irregular eating patterns?
Do you experience bloating, fatigue after eating, or poor appetite?

tcm_patterns.description: In TCM, the Kidneys govern the bones and teeth. Dreams of teeth falling out may reflect declining Kidney Jing (essence), especially with aging.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Teeth crumbling, falling out easily, fear of aging

tcm_patterns.description: Stomach heat rising to the gums can also manifest as tooth dreams. The upper gums relate to the Stomach meridian.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Teeth aching before falling, bleeding gums in dream, sensation of heat

follow_up_questions: Were the teeth crumbling or being pulled?
Did your gums bleed in the dream?
Do you have any dental issues or grind your teeth?
Do you experience lower back pain, frequent urination, or premature aging signs?

tcm_patterns.description: Snakes in TCM symbolism relate to internal wind -- their sinuous movement mirrors the unpredictable, zigzagging nature of Liver wind.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Moving, striking snakes; sudden appearance; feeling of danger

tcm_patterns.description: Snakes can also represent deep, primal fear stored in the Kidney system.
tcm_patterns.distinguishing_features: Hidden snakes, lurking in dark water, coiled threats

follow_up_questions: Was the snake moving or still?
Did it threaten you or were you observing it?
Was it in water or on land?
Do you experience tremors, dizziness, or sudden headaches?

classical_herbal_formulas.description: Classical TCM formulas historically used for dream-disturbed sleep. These are for reference only and should be prescribed by a qualified TCM practitioner.

formulas.name_en: Banxia Shumi Tang (Pinellia and Millet Decoction)
formulas.indication: Stomach qi disharmony causing insomnia and disturbed dreams. The original formula from the Nei Jing for dream-disturbed sleep.
formulas.pattern: Stomach qi failing to descend, yin and yang unable to exchange

formulas.source: Ji Sheng Fang (Formulas to Aid the Living)
formulas.indication: Heart-Spleen deficiency with excessive dreaming, poor memory, fatigue, and anxiety. The most common formula for overthinking-related dream disturbance.
formulas.pattern: Heart blood and Spleen qi deficiency

formulas.source: Bei Ji Qian Jin Yao Fang
formulas.indication: Phlegm-heat disturbing the Heart causing nightmares, restless sleep, and vivid frightening dreams.

formulas.source: Jin Gui Yao Lue (Synopsis of the Golden Chamber)
formulas.indication: Liver blood deficiency with heat causing insomnia, vivid dreams, irritability, and night sweats.
formulas.pattern: Liver blood deficiency with deficiency heat

formulas.name_en: Tianwang Buxin Dan (Heavenly Emperor's Heart-Supplementing Pill)
formulas.indication: Heart-Kidney yin deficiency with insomnia, excessive dreaming, palpitations, poor memory, and night sweats.

formulas.name_en: Xuefu Zhuyu Tang (Drive Out Blood Stasis from the Mansion of Blood Decoction)
formulas.indication: Blood stasis causing chronic insomnia with dark, disturbing dreams that recur night after night.
formulas.pattern: Blood stasis in the chest obstructing Heart qi

universal_acupressure_protocol.description: A simple self-care acupressure routine for improving sleep quality and reducing dream disturbance. Can be performed nightly before bed.

points.name_en: Yin Tang (Hall of Impression / Third Eye)
points.technique: Press gently with middle finger or thumb. Hold for 1-2 minutes with slow, deep breathing.
points.benefit: Calms the Shen, induces relaxation, clears the mind. Excellent for anxiety-driven dream disturbance.

points.location: Wrist crease, pinky side, in the depression at the radial side of the pisiform bone
points.technique: Press with opposite thumb for 1-2 minutes per wrist.
points.benefit: The master point for calming the Spirit. Treats insomnia, nightmares, and excessive dreaming.

points.location: Inner forearm, 2 inches above wrist crease, between the two tendons
points.technique: Press with thumb for 1-2 minutes per arm.
points.benefit: Calms the Heart, unbinds the chest, alleviates palpitations and anxiety.

points.location: Inner leg, 3 inches above the ankle bone, behind the tibia
points.technique: Press with thumb for 1-2 minutes per leg. Contraindicated during pregnancy.
points.benefit: Nourishes yin of Liver, Kidney, and Spleen simultaneously. One of the most important points for sleep.

points.location: Sole of the foot, in the depression when toes are curled
points.technique: Rub vigorously with palm for 2-3 minutes per foot while lying in bed.
points.benefit: Draws excess yang and heat downward from the head. Grounds the spirit. Calms fear.

points.location: Behind the ear, midpoint between Yifeng (SJ17) and Fengchi (GB20), in the depression behind the mastoid process
points.technique: Press gently with fingertips for 1 minute per side.
points.benefit: The name literally means Peaceful Sleep. Specific for insomnia and dream-disturbed sleep.

ayurveda_dream_types.description: Ayurvedic dream interpretation based on the three doshas. Included for cross-cultural reference and for users who resonate with the Ayurvedic framework.
ayurveda_dream_types.core_principle: In Ayurveda, dreams (Svapna) reflect the dreamer's constitutional type (Prakriti) and current imbalance (Vikriti). The content, quality, and emotional tone of dreams indicate which dosha is predominant or disturbed.

dream_classification.drishta: Dreams based on things seen or experienced in waking life
dream_classification.anubhuta: Dreams based on things experienced through other senses
dream_classification.prarthita: Dreams based on desires or wishes
dream_classification.bhavika: Prophetic dreams that may indicate future events or prognosis
dream_classification.doshaja: Dreams caused by dosha imbalance (most diagnostically relevant)

doshas.qualities: Light, dry, cold, mobile, subtle, rough
balanced_dream_characteristics: Creative, imaginative dreams with vivid imagery
imbalanced_dream_themes: Dreams that shift rapidly and feel chaotic
doshas.dream_quality: Light, fragmented, quickly forgotten. May wake frequently during the night. Dreams feel chaotic, fast-moving, and hard to follow.

dietary: Warm, moist, grounding foods (soups, stews, warm milk)
Ghee, sesame oil, warm spices (cinnamon, ginger, cardamom)
Warm golden milk (turmeric milk) before bed
lifestyle: Establish a consistent daily routine (dinacharya)
Oil massage (Abhyanga) with warm sesame oil before bed
Keep the bedroom warm and comfortable
Avoid stimulation before sleep (screens, intense conversation)
Practice grounding yoga: forward folds, seated poses
herbal: Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) for calming and grounding
Warm milk with nutmeg before bed

doshas.qualities: Hot, sharp, light, liquid, oily, spreading
balanced_dream_characteristics: Teaching or leading others in dreams
imbalanced_dream_themes: Sun, bright light, meteors, shooting stars
Red flowers, red-colored trees and objects
doshas.dream_quality: Vivid, colorful, intense, and well-remembered. Often involves conflict or achievement. May wake feeling hot or angry. Dreams have strong narrative structure.

dietary: Avoid spicy, sour, and fermented foods
lifestyle: Avoid vigorous exercise in the evening
Moonlight walks for cooling energy
Practice cooling pranayama (Shitali / Sitkari breathing)
Keep the bedroom cool and dark
Avoid competitive activities before bed
herbal: Shatavari (Asparagus racemosus) for cooling and calming
Rose water applied to forehead and temples

doshas.qualities: Heavy, slow, cool, oily, smooth, stable, soft
balanced_dream_characteristics: Dreams of calm water, gardens, nature
imbalanced_dream_themes: Swimming in the sea or a sea of milk
doshas.dream_quality: Long, deep, slow-moving dreams. Often replay memories or familiar scenes. May feel excessively pleasant, even indulgent. Can feel trapped in long dreams. Hard to wake from.

dietary: Light, warm, dry foods with pungent, bitter, and astringent tastes
Spicy foods (ginger, black pepper, cayenne) to stimulate metabolism
Honey in warm water (not hot) in the morning
Avoid heavy, oily, sweet, and cold foods
lifestyle: Wake early (before 6 AM) to avoid Kapha sluggishness
Vigorous morning exercise (running, dancing, active yoga)
Dry brushing (Garshana) to stimulate circulation
Avoid daytime napping
Engage in stimulating, varied activities
herbal: Trikatu (ginger, black pepper, long pepper) for stimulating digestion
Guggulu for clearing congestion and stagnation
Tulsi (Holy Basil) tea for clarity and warmth
Triphala before bed for gentle cleansing

tcm_ayurveda_correspondences.description: Approximate mapping between TCM and Ayurvedic frameworks for cross-system reference. These are not exact equivalences but useful parallels.

mappings.shared_themes: Anger, frustration, ambition, heat rising, transformation
mappings.note: Liver yang rising shares qualities with Pitta excess: heat, intensity, irritability

mappings.shared_themes: Fire imagery, heat, intensity, inflammation, sharp emotions
mappings.note: Heart Fire and Pitta aggravation both manifest as fire dreams, agitation, and heat signs
Spleen dampness and Kapha excess share qualities of heaviness, sluggishness, and accumulation

mappings.shared_themes: Dryness, grief, letting go, breath, the boundary between self and world
mappings.note: Both systems link respiratory function to emotional processing of loss

mappings.shared_themes: Fear, depletion, aging, cold, foundational vitality
mappings.note: Kidney deficiency and Vata derangement share exhaustion, fear, dryness, and cold

ai_interview_guidance.description: Guidance for the AI dream interview system on how to use TCM patterns in dream analysis.
when_to_apply: When dream content matches specific organ patterns (fire, water, flying, falling, anger, grief, etc.)
When the user reports recurring dreams of the same type
When the user mentions physical symptoms alongside their dreams
When the dream occurs during a specific season that amplifies the organ pattern
When the user expresses interest in TCM or holistic health perspectives
ai_interview_guidance.integration_approach: TCM dream interpretation should be offered as one perspective alongside psychological (Jungian, cognitive) and cultural interpretations. It should never replace medical advice but can complement it by connecting dream content to holistic wellness awareness.
sensitivity_notes: Present TCM concepts as a traditional framework, not as definitive medical diagnosis
Use accessible language while preserving authentic terminology
Offer wellness suggestions as invitations, not prescriptions
Acknowledge that dream interpretation is inherently subjective across all traditions
Respect the user's cultural background; offer Ayurvedic parallels when relevant

example_integration.dream_input: I dreamed I was drowning in dark, cold water and couldn't reach the surface
example_integration.tcm_layer: In Traditional Chinese Medicine, water dreams -- especially drowning in cold, dark water -- are associated with the Kidney system (肾). The Kidney governs water, stores our essential vitality (Jing), and houses the emotion of fear. This dream pattern may reflect a period of deep exhaustion, fear, or feeling overwhelmed by life's depths. It is especially significant if it occurs in winter, the Kidney's season. Consider: Are you pushing yourself beyond your reserves? Simple self-care includes rubbing the soles of your feet (Yong Quan / KD1 point) before bed and eating warming, Kidney-nourishing foods like black sesame, walnuts, and bone broth.
